question: How did he tell you it was safe enough to leave for the mountains?
answer:
He sent me a note that said, "Come with this guy (he was a Peshmerga) and bring some cash money with you. Hide it under your Kurdish outfit. Wear raggy clothes, don't wear makeup, and wear tennis shoes. Pretend like you are from the village."

I didn't know if it was safe or not; he didn't tell me it was safe. He just said, "Come and I will meet you on the other side of the Goyzha Mountain."

I left in the evening, around six or seven o'clock. I went together with this guy, climbing the mountain, walking, tired, and hungry. That was it.
